name: EMP Field
effect: A compact emitter that generates a focused burst of electromagnetic energy. When activated as an action, all technology, ciphers, and electrical systems within a 20-foot radius are immediately Soft Anchored and disabled for 1 round.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** The field overloads, triggering **Synaptic Freeze** on up to *Perception Modifier + Neuronal Capacity Modifier* targets, freezing their Synaptic Space and shutting down all tech for 2 rounds.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** High-frequency feedback causes **Cipher Backlash**: the wielder's SS costs are increased to 1.8x, and all opponents gain Action Priority.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires spending a Prepared Action in combat or completing a Recovery Phase.

name: Bio-Monitor
effect: *Balanced to prevent metagame exploits.* Provides real-time tactical readouts of vital signs, pathogen levels, and active status conditions of any target within a 30-foot Zone of Influence (ZoI). This grants a flat **+5 bonus on all medical triage rolls** made with a Field Trauma Kit.

name: Accelerant Drug (Consumable)
effect: An adrenaline-derived injector that temporarily accelerates synapses. Consuming this as a bonus action grants the user **+1 Action Density** and **+2 Synaptic Space** for 2 rounds.

name: Hyper Accelerant Drug
effect: *Reworked to balance physical scaling.* A high-potency, neuro-chemical booster. When consumed as a bonus action, it grants **+2 Action Density**, **+4 Dexterity**, and **+3 Neuronal Capacity** for 2 rounds.

name: Holographic Projector
effect: Allows the user to project 3D tactical wireframe maps or communications models in real-time. Grants a +5 bonus to tactical planning checks during Calm situation states.

name: Drone Of Compassion (D.O.C.)
effect: A compact hovering drone that tracks allied life-signs. It can passively stabilize adjacent dying allies or administer a single use of a Field Trauma Kit as an off-turn bonus action.

name: Cryo-Sleep Pod
effect: *Reworked to prevent escape exploits.* A portable, folding cryogenic chamber. If an ally is dying or critically poisoned, placing them in the pod suspends all bleed and toxin timers indefinitely. While stored, the target is **Hard Anchored** and completely immune to external status effects, but cannot act.

name: Extractor
effect: *Balanced for material acquisition.* Automatically mines nearby elemental nodes or scrap piles during In-game Time Tracking (ITT). Increases raw resource yields by exactly **+50%** and reduces harvesting time by half.

name: Replicator
effect: *Balanced to prevent infinite economy loops.* Can scan and store the blueprints of standard consumables (e.g., Trauma Kits, Drugs). By spending scrap or raw materials equal to the item's grade value during a Recovery Phase, the Replicator generates one identical copy of the consumable. Cannot copy weapons, modules, or cybernetics.

name: Null-Woven Piercers
effect: Specialized micro-actuators fitted to standard weapon barrels. Allows exactly **25% of all physical damage** dealt by the equipped weapon to bypass the target's Defensive Hit Points (DHP) and resistances to strike HP directly.

name: Zero-Cycle Encapsulator
effect: Projects a chronological stasis shield around the wielder, blocking exterior environmental hazards.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Initiates **Spatial Propagation**. For 1 round, the wielder is immune to all negative status effects and conditions. Any condition applied to them is transferred to another target within 35 feet.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Severe neural drainage causes **Genus Backlash**: SS costs double, and all combatants override the user's Action Priority during their next action.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action to re-stabilize the temporal generator.

name: H.Z. Compact Zone Absorber
effect: A deployable, see-through protective barrier that blocks incoming projectiles. The barrier has **20 DHP** and deactivates once depleted.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Triggers **Vector Echo**. The shield expands, and any kinetic projectile striking it is reflected back at the attacker (dealing 1.5x reduced damage).
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** The electric surge triggers **Genus Backlash**, leaving the wielder's Action Priority at the lowest position.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action to reboot the shielding matrix.

name: G.M. Bouncers
effect: Gravity-manipulating boot thrusters. Allows the user to perform a mid-air double jump, or ignore movement penalties while operating in Space Combat environments.

name: Truthseeker’s Vision
effect: A sensor visor that parses biometric markers. Once per scene, active it to bypass a social Cunning/Perception check; you automatically detect if a target within 10 feet is lying.

name: Oriyu Core
effect: A core derived from the Oriyu species. When the wielder is hit by an energy or Genus-based attack, they reduce all subsequent damage of that specific energy type by exactly **10%** (stacks up to three times per encounter).

name: Liars Voice-Box
effect: A vocal modulator that twists spoken audio waves to project false intent.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Resolves **Requirement Inversion**. The wielder rewrites a previously spoken sentence, forcing any listener to reroll their Insight or Perception checks against a contested *Action Priority + Intelligence* roll.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Neural feedback triggers **Cipher Backlash**, inflating the user's Synaptic Space costs by 1.8x.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action to reset the vocal filter.

name: Spatial Locking Pole
effect: A collapsible pole that can anchor itself in mid-air or onto any surface, creating an instant structural platform. Supports up to 500 lbs and prevents falling when held.

name: Prosopocoilus F.B.
effect: A stabilizer harness that locks the joints under impact. The user is immune to the first instance of being knocked prone, tripped, or forced into a Soft Anchor state per encounter.

name: 6L3PH4N7
effect:
*Balanced to fit combat action density.* A deployable heavy call-box that summons a massive, metal-plated automated elephant construct to drop onto a target area within 30 feet. 
- **Summon Effect:** The construct crushes down, dealing **1d40 Crushing damage** to all targets in a 10-foot radius and knocking them prone. It remains on the field for 3 turns, acting as a massive hard-anchor obstacle that blocks sight and provides full cover.

overclock.text:
- **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Under the Kinetic Domain, the impact triggers **Vector Echo**: the impact deals a second wave of 1d40 Crushing damage (reduced by 1.5x) and extends the construct's operational lifespan to 5 turns.
- **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Neural load triggers **Genus Backlash**, doubling the wielder's SS costs for 1 round.
- **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action in combat to clear the remote signal link.

name: Cyber-Optical Vibrant Camo
effect: Bending light to conceal the operator. Grants a +10 bonus to Stealth checks. The camo drops immediately upon attacking, casting a Cipher, or entering a hostile creature's 30-foot Zone of Influence (ZoI).

name: Genus Impacter
effect: A specialized grenade containing concentrated Genus energy (can be Null, Eldritch, Neutral, Elemental, or Kinetic). When thrown up to 30 feet, it deals **2d6 Explosive damage** of the chosen Genus energy type in a 10-foot blast radius.

name: Energy Absorbing Rebounder
effect: A spherical device that absorbs ambient kinetic and energy attacks, converting them into charge. If the wielder takes energy damage, the sphere gains 1 charge (max 5). As an action, the sphere can be thrown to release a shockwave dealing **1d8 Force damage per charge** in a 15-foot radius.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Overdrives the containment core, triggering **Environmental Overdrive**. This doubles the blast radius (30 feet), the damage dice size (1d10 per charge), and inflicts an ongoing elemental status burn on all targets.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Overheating triggers **Genus Backlash**, temporarily overriding the user's Action Priority during the combat window.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires completing a Recovery Phase or spending a Prepared Action to vent the thermal cell.

name: Audio-Sound Blastcore
effect: A deployable sound box that blasts localized, high-decibel acoustic interference. Once activated, all creatures within 15 feet take **1d8 Sonic damage** per turn and are deafened (cannot receive remote communications, and their Perception Range is reduced to zero).

name: A.F. Teleportation Device
effect: *Balanced with strict spatial limits.* A pair of quantum-linked spatial disks. Placing one disk as an action allows the user to teleport any Medium or smaller object or willing creature between the two disks as a Prepared Action. Range is capped at exactly **60 feet**.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Triggers **Spatial Propagation**. The wielder can instantly swap positions with a hostile creature within 60 feet. Resolving this requires succeeding on a contested *Action Priority + Intelligence check*.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Neural dislocation causes **Cipher Backlash**: Synaptic Space costs are increased to 1.8x, and all opponents act first.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires completing a Recovery Phase to reset the quantum entanglement.

name: BIOS Marker
effect: A scanning pulse emitter. When thrown up to 30 feet, it marks all biological targets within a 15-foot radius. Marked targets remain visible through solid walls and cover to all allies for 3 rounds.

name: Boots of Flapping
effect: *Preserved joke item with funny niche benefit.* Features small synthetic wings that flap noisily whenever the Inquisitor jumps. Grants **+1 Evasion** specifically and only while airborne (Aerial state). Under normal circumstances, "provides no realistic bonuses while flapping".

name: Invisibility Cloak
effect: *Balanced to limit infinite defense.* A cloak that renders all equipped items, armor, and clothing completely invisible, leaving the user's body slightly transparent. This grants a flat **+10 bonus to Stealth checks**, but imposes **-1 Evasion** because the user cannot easily visually track their own armor boundaries in a melee fight.

name: Vial of Oxygen (Consumable)
effect: A highly pressurized oxygen canister. Grants the user exactly **12 rounds of continuous breathing** while submerged, exposed to void space, or choked by pathogens.

name: Second Wind
effect: An automated subcutaneous defibrillator. If the user is knocked unconscious, the device discharges an electrical pulse, instantly reviving them with **1 HP**. This charge is spent and can only be restored during a Recovery Phase.

name: Domain Marker
effect: A proximity-based sensor post. When placed, if any entity enters a 20-foot radius around the marker, it triggers a silent signal to the user's HUD.

name: Power Drainer
effect: A contact-based siphoning device. When held against any piece of active tech or cybernetic hardware, it drains its battery, disabling it for 1 round or reducing its NC cost by half.

name: Gravity Lifter
effect: A deployable launching pad. Any creature stepping onto it is launched **10 meters** vertically into the air. For the next round, their gravity is halved, granting them the **Aerial Combat State** (+1 Action Priority, -1 Balance).

name: T1-AUT0 Turret
effect:
A deployable automated combat turret. Once placed as an action, it automatically targets non-allied creatures within 30 feet, dealing **1d8 Bludgeoning damage** per turn on its own Action Priority window.
These high-grade logic gates occupy the **MODULE** slots of an Inquisitor's terminal, modifying character variables.

name: Malfunctioning Module
effect: *Balanced for risky play.* A faulty chip featuring a deck of unpredictable possibilities. Once per encounter, as a free action, roll a d6:

name: Module: Crown of the JOAT
effect: *Balanced generalist node.* The Jack-of-all-Trades module. It provides a flat **+1 modifier to all 12 specialties**. Excellent for generalist builds but highly taxing on your available Neural Capacity.

name: Module: Tesseract
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Passively increases all base stats by grade value. (Broken: Bypasses 20 stat cap, completely breaking derived statistics scaling math).
*   *Balanced Rework:* The Tesseract is re-engineered as a **pocket-dimension storage module**. It bends spatial gravity, reducing your total equipment Weight by -5. It allows storing up to 10 standard items in a pocket dimension, which do not contribute to your load limits or Movement/Evasion penalties.

name: Sandevistan Neural Link
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Ability to increase perception of time and increase the speed of body. (Broken: Under-defined, easily leads to infinite combat actions).
*   *Balanced Rework:* A cranial co-processor that hyper-accelerates neural pathways.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** The wielder enters a state of localized temporal acceleration for 1 round. They gain **+2 Action Density** and their Movement is doubled.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Synaptic exhaustion causes **Genus Backlash**: SS costs double, and the user is instantly **Winded** (Evasion is halved, and they take 1d8 Winded damage).
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires spending a Prepared Action in combat or completing a Recovery Phase.

name: Nano-machine
effect: Subcutaneous implants that harden in response to physical trauma.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Triggers **Vector Echo**. The nano-machines align to absorb kinetic impact. For 1 round, any Bludgeoning, Slashing, or Piercing attack dealt to the user has its damage reduced by 50%, and the impact resolves as a shockwave that deals Bludgeoning damage to enemies in contact.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Hardening creates systemic stiffness, triggering **Genus Backlash**: Evasion and Action Priority are severely overridden by all active combatants.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action to vent the neural stiffness.

name: Nano-mites
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Repairs anything done to destabilize/destroy body back to peak condition. (Broken: Infinite passive healing completely bypasses combat stakes).
*   *Balanced Rework:* Active micro-colonies that purge foreign toxins and repair cells.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Triggers **Environmental Overdrive**. Instantly purges all active pathogens, poisons, or bleeding status conditions, and restores exactly **10 HP** per round for 2 rounds.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Cellular exhaustion triggers **Genus Backlash**: SS costs double, and Action Priority is overridden.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a full Recovery Phase outside of combat to re-harvest cellular colonies.

name: Kiroshi Optics
effect: Ocular implants that overlay biometric data, heat signatures, and zooming vectors.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Resolves **Requirement Inversion**. The optics calculate precise blind-spots. The user ignores enemy cover bonuses and physical obstructions, granting a +5 bonus to their next attack roll.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Neural saturation causes **Cipher Backlash**, inflating SS costs to 1.8x.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a Prepared Action to recalibrate visual matrices.

name: Epimorphic Skeleton
effect: bioplastic bone reinforcement. Grants flat **-2 damage reduction** against all physical Blunt attacks.

name: Titanium Bones/Plating
effect: Metallic reinforcement of bone matrices. The user ignores weapon recoil penalties and gains advantage on Strength checks to lift or carry.

name: Subdermal Plating
effect: Sub-epidermal armor mesh. Grants a permanent **-2 damage reduction** against Slashing and Piercing damage.

name: Energy Subdermal Plating
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Plating integrated inside the skin. Energized to absorb energy attacks and reduce damage or charge batteries. (Broken: Infinite scaling resource absorb).
*   *Balanced Rework:* Synthetic skin overlay engineered with conductive gel matrices. Grants permanent **-2 damage reduction** against physical Blunt, Slashing, and Piercing damage. Additionally, when struck by a Fire, Cold, or Lightning attack, the wielder absorbs the thermal energy to instantly **restore 1 spent Neural Capacity** (max once per round).

name: Bionic Limbs
effect: Biomechanical replacement limb. Melee strikes using the bionic limb deal standard Bludgeoning 1d6 damage, and the limb cannot be fractured or severed.

name: Lynx Paws
effect: Acoustic dampening pads installed in the feet. Grants a flat **+8 bonus on all Stealth checks** and renders the user's footsteps completely silent.

name: Advanced Respiratory Filter
effect: An intranasal filter mesh. The user is completely immune to airborne pathogens, venom/poison gases, or inhalation-based sedatives.

name: Fortified Legs
effect: Pneumatic leg actuators. The user's jump height is doubled, and they ignore the first **10 meters** of any fall damage.

name: Brain Implant: Enhanced Capabilities
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Half of your brain is converted to machine to boost all cognitive skills. (Broken: Bypasses specialty and Attribute caps).
*   *Balanced Rework:* Co-processors grafted into the cerebral cortex. It boosts baseline cognitive attributes (**+2 Intelligence, +2 Wisdom**), but the loss of emotional nuance reduces social capability (**-2 Charisma**).

name: Brain Implant: Converse
effect: A sub-dermal radio receiver that allows secure telepathic communication with any selected target within 60 feet.

name: Brain Implant: Vitals
effect: An internal diagnostic HUD. Allows the operator to view their exact HP, DHP, active pathogens, and poison thresholds in real-time.

name: Brain Implant: Integrated Database
effect: Grafts a localized copy of the Inquisitor Vault database onto the memory sectors. Allows the user to instantly identify biological species, corporate ranks, or physical traits of targets within 30 feet.

name: Neural-Link Override
effect:
*   *Original Concept:* Manually override the cooldown of a genus or cipher to force another use. (Broken: Bypasses combat balance, causes infinite casts).
*   *Balanced Rework:* A highly dangerous shunt that forcibly drains physical HP to override neural limits.

overclock.text:
*   **Phase I (Extender Phase):** Resolves **Requirement Inversion**. As a free action, you manually bypass an active Genus or Cipher cooldown to cast it again. Doing so immediately inflicts **1d10 True Damage** to your HP (bypassing DHP) and triggers **Cipher Backlash**, raising future SS costs to 1.8x.
*   **Phase II (Backlash Phase):** Neural saturation triggers **Genus Backlash**, doubling the wielder's SS costs, and all active combatants override their Action Priority for 1 round.
*   **Phase III (Equalizer Phase):** Requires a full Recovery Phase outside of combat to re-attune the burned synaptic shunts.
